steps.title: Margin of error defines a confidence interval
steps.content:
The margin of error tells us the range around the sample mean where the true population mean is expected to fall.

We construct this interval by adding and subtracting the margin of error from the sample mean:
$$\text{Confidence interval} = [\text{Sample mean} - \text{Margin of error}, \text{Sample mean} + \text{Margin of error}]$$

steps.title: Calculate the interval bounds
steps.content:
Substitute the given values:
$$\text{Lower bound} = 3.28 - 0.06 = 3.22$$
$$\text{Upper bound} = 3.28 + 0.06 = 3.34$$
The confidence interval is [\$3.22, \$3.34].

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "It is between $3.22 and $3.34.": This is exactly the confidence interval — a plausible range for the true population mean. ✓
  • Choice B — "It is either less than $3.22 or greater than $3.34.": This asserts the true mean falls outside the confidence interval, which is implausible. ✗
  • Choice C — "It is less than $3.22.": Only the far lower tail — not supported by the data. ✗
  • Choice D — "It is greater than $3.34.": Only the far upper tail — not supported by the data. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
